tagline: Calculate concrete volume and bags for slabs, patios, and driveways
title: Concrete Slab Calculator — How Many Yards & Bags Do I Need?
metaDescription: Calculate cubic yards and bags of concrete for slabs, patios, driveways, and sidewalks. Enter dimensions, get instant volume and cost estimates.
keywords: concrete slab calculator, how much concrete do I need, concrete yardage calculator, concrete bag calculator, patio concrete calculator, driveway concrete
calculatorHTML:
Concrete Slab Calculator

Quick Presets

Sidewalks: 4" • Patios: 4" • Garages: 6" • Footings: 8"+
Ready-mix: $100–$150/yd³ delivered
Cubic Yards
—
Cubic Feet

howItWorks.intro: Calculating concrete for a slab is simple: multiply length, width, and depth to get volume, then convert to cubic yards — the standard ordering unit for ready-mix concrete.
rules: Measure length and width in feet, depth in inches. Formula: L(ft) × W(ft) × D(in)/12 = cubic feet, then divide by 27 for cubic yards
Standard slab depth is 4 inches for patios and sidewalks, 6 inches for garages and driveways with heavy traffic
Always order 5-10% extra concrete to account for spillage, uneven subgrade, and form movement
howItWorks.outro: Ready-mix concrete is sold by the cubic yard. Most trucks carry 8-10 cubic yards. For small jobs under 1 yard, bagged concrete (60 lb or 80 lb bags) may be more practical. This calculator shows both options.

faq.question: How much concrete do I need for a 10x10 slab?
faq.answer: A 10x10 slab at 4 inches thick requires 1.24 cubic yards of concrete (about 33 cubic feet). That's roughly 56 bags of 80-lb concrete mix or 1.3 yards of ready-mix. Always order 5-10% extra for waste and spillage.

faq.question: How many bags of concrete are in a cubic yard?
faq.answer: One cubic yard of concrete equals approximately 45 bags of 80-lb concrete mix or 60 bags of 60-lb mix. For jobs over 1 cubic yard, ordering ready-mix delivery is usually more cost-effective and easier than mixing bags by hand.

faq.question: How thick should a concrete slab be?
faq.answer: Sidewalks and patios: 4 inches. Garages and driveways: 4-6 inches. Heavy equipment pads: 6-8 inches. The slab should rest on 4-6 inches of compacted gravel base for proper drainage and to prevent cracking.

faq.question: How much does a yard of concrete cost?
faq.answer: Ready-mix concrete costs $100-$150 per cubic yard delivered, depending on your region and the mix design. Short-load fees ($15-$50 per yard under the minimum) apply for orders under 3-5 yards. An 80-lb bag costs about $5-$7, which works out to roughly $225-$315 per cubic yard.

faq.question: Do I need rebar or wire mesh in my slab?
faq.answer: For most residential slabs, welded wire mesh (6x6 W1.4/W1.4) provides adequate reinforcement. Use #4 rebar on 24-inch centers for garage floors, driveways, or any slab over 5 inches thick. Fiber-reinforced concrete is another option for crack control in patios and walkways.

faq.question: How long does concrete take to cure?
faq.answer: Concrete reaches about 70% of its full strength in 7 days and 99% in 28 days. You can walk on it after 24-48 hours and drive on it after 7 days. Keep it moist for the first 7 days by misting or covering with plastic for best results.
